fresh_facts.claim: The US deployed approximately 2,500 Marines from the 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit aboard the USS Tripoli and other amphibious ships to the Middle East toward the Strait of Hormuz as of March 19, 2026.
US military conducted bombing raids on Iran's Kharg Island, Iran's primary oil export terminal, which President Trump described as one of the most powerful raids in Middle East history.
fresh_facts.source: Social media statement by President Trump

fresh_facts.claim: Iranian military escalated missile and drone attacks on Persian Gulf shipping and energy infrastructure, including shrapnel falling in over 600 locations in Qatar despite most interceptions.
Iranian IRGC forces closed the Strait of Hormuz effectively through military operations, which carries 20% of global oil and drove up global oil prices.
A large explosion occurred in Tehran's Quds Day square amid anti-US/Israel chants, though its cause remains unclear from Iranian state TV reports.
Trump highlighted the Basij paramilitary force's role in crushing protests with machine guns as a major hurdle to Iranian opposition toppling the regime.
fresh_facts.source: Social media statement by President Trump

fresh_facts.claim: Iran's regime claims its political structure remains operational following the killing of Ali Larijani and other top figures as of March 18, 2026.
Mojtaba Khamenei's inner circle is dominated by hardline IRGC commanders, with his first message emphasizing war continuation, new fronts, and Basij street presence.
Hardline IRGC elements dominate decision-making in Iran after Ali Khamenei's killing, with no evidence of fractures or defections despite strikes on leadership compounds.
Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi engaged in defensive diplomacy denying civilian targeting and proposing joint probes with neighbors as of March 15, 2026.
Albania's PM Edi Rama planned a parliamentary vote during the week of March 13 to declare Iran a state terrorism sponsor.
Iran threatened to close the Strait of Hormuz and demand neighbor 'compensation' as crisis export as of March 15, 2026.
Drone attacks hit IRGC and Basij checkpoints in Tehran and other Iranian cities, killing forces including the Tehran Basij commander as of March 15, 2026.
Anti-IRGC chants with slogans such as 'death to the IRGC' were reported in the Saadat Abad area of Iran as of March 15, 2026.
IRGC Intelligence warned of 'enemy' protests and ordered Basij street presence in multiple Iranian cities as of March 12, 2026.
IRGC checkpoints multiplied in Iranian cities with forces scanning skies as of March 15, 2026.
Strikes on detention facilities in Mariwan caused prisoner jailbreaks in early March 2026.
IRGC ordered western Iranian city evacuations in early March 2026, signaling ground military preparation.
Israeli strikes killed senior IRGC intelligence figures including Abdollah Jalali-Nasab around March 15, 2026.
Hamas publicly urged Iran to avoid targeting Arab neighbors as of March 15, 2026.
VP Vance noted uncertainty on whether a strike injuring Iran's new Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei was US or Israeli-led.
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth ordered the deployment of 2,500 Marines to the Middle East, approved by US Central Command.
The Strait of Hormuz carries 20% of global oil, making Iranian disruptions to shipping a significant economic threat.

regime_fall.current_status: Following Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei's death and Mojtaba Khamenei's succession, the Iranian regime faces sustained military pressure from coordinated US-Israeli operations including strikes on Kharg Island oil infrastructure, deployment of 2,500 US Marines toward the Strait of Hormuz, and Iranian closure of the Strait in response. Internally, the regime is experiencing simultaneous instability with anti-IRGC protests, drone strikes on security checkpoints, and prisoner jailbreaks, while Mojtaba's hardline IRGC-dominated inner circle maintains control through expanded Basij deployment and repression.
regime_fall.last_major_event: US military conducted bombing raids on Iran's Kharg Island primary oil export terminal on March 13, 2026, described by President Trump as one of the most powerful raids in Middle East history, while the 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit with 2,500 Marines deployed toward the Strait of Hormuz as of March 19, 2026.
